items.title: Art created for NT school reading program goes on display in Canberra

children.content: More than 40 years ago, a program was created to help Papunya children in remote NT learn to read in Pintupi-Luritja languages. Those books are now an insight into the people, stories, history and culture of the Papunya and are on display at the National Library of Australia.

gallery.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

thumbnail.altText: A yellow book cover that reads "Ngaatja Nyaalytjingka?".

items.title: Crucial exhibits unveiled to jury in Irma Palasics cold case trial

children.content: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

image.altText: A fridge with many containers in it.

items.title: Low branch reported two weeks before cyclist's death, coronial reveals

children.content: A cyclist’s death on a popular Canberra path could have been prevented if the ACT government had acted promptly on a public warning about a dangerous overhanging branch, coronial findings reveal.

image.altText: A cyclist riding on an outdoor path.

items.title: 'Cameras watching': Horrifying trend of technology-based abuse

children.content: One in three women on temporary visas are experiencing domestic and family violence, safety advocates say.

gallery.altText: A professional with glasses and a sombre expression.

items.title: Public hearing into hiring of new CIT CEO cancelled as probe continues

children.content: The chair of the CIT board told an ACT Legislative Assembly inquiry the complaints made against chief executive Margot McNeill related to perceptions of conflicts of interest around employments and contracts.

image.altText: A woman with classes in a green suit.

items.title: Draft report into ANU's controversial cuts critical of university's council

children.content: ANU's council approved a massive $250 million cost-cutting program without clear evidence it was needed or achievable, according to an assessment in a draft audit office report.

image.altText: Signage for the ANU.

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

gallery.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

thumbnail.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence

children.content: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

A sign on a fence warning people not to enter.
A handful of men in military uniform behind a truck.

children.content: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

children.content: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

items.title: I find myself weeping again for Lebanon

children.content: Lebanese families have been told to flee homes that they might never be able to return to, and families are setting up tents for an uncertain and dangerous life on the streets of Beirut all because of a conflict not of their making, brought about by another state.

image.altText: Women walk by a damaged building in Beirut.

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: How nightmare Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle became a historic storm

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

image.altText: Timelapse of Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path

items.title: From Apollo to Artemis: Re-used rocket parts but brand new toilets

children.content: More than 50 years ago, the Apollo missions took humanity to the Moon. Now with Artemis, a new generation is about to be launched. What has changed — and not changed — between then and now might surprise you.

image.altText: Black and white photo of woman with long hair, glasses and a long-sleeved dress next to a stack of bound papers as tall as her.

items.title: Cops would not have had 'luxury' of taking Dezi Freeman alive, experts say

children.content: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

items.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

children.content: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

items.title: Nasal spray flu vaccines are here — but not all kids can get it for free

children.content: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

items.title: 10 podcasts we can't stop listening to at the moment

items.title: FIFA raises top ticket price for World Cup final to nearly $16,000

items.title: Woods heard telling police he was 'just talking to the president' after crash

children.content: Police have released body camera footage showing Tiger Woods's arrest after he crashed his SUV last week in Florida.

items.title: A 'rare parasitic fungus' is infecting Top End footballers. Here's what we know

children.content: Health authorities say a rare water-borne pathogen is behind the recent outbreak of severe conjunctivitis in Top End football clubs, with a number of Darwin playing fields deemed high-risk infection sites.
